factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: Major headline: Iran-US peace deal framework near completion with Hormuz set to reopen; multiple sources confirm signing in coming days. Risk-off de-escalation typically weighs on safe-haven demand for gold, though initial market reaction showed modest upside as inflation anxiety persists.

factors.name: Oil Price (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: US crude fell below $85 on Friday as Iran deal progress signaled Hormuz reopening; lower oil reduces near-term inflation input and removes geopolitical premium. This eases Fed pressure, which is initially gold-negative but longer-term bullish via lower real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY lost ground during the week (down 0.27% to ~99.80) ahead of heavy central bank calendar; risk-on sentiment on Iran deal framework typically weakens the dollar. Weaker USD is a direct structural bullish factor for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news window, but lower nominal yields expected if geopolitical premium unwinds and Fed holds or cuts. Without fresh CPI/inflation data, real yields remain the dominant unknown—critical to monitor for next 24-72h.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: No FOMC decision or Fed speak in latest news cycle. Upcoming central bank calendar (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions) noted for next week; no hawkish surprise yet. This is a key upcoming catalyst that could shift bias sharply if Fed signals higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: Inflation Data (CPI/PCE/PPI)
factors.summary: No recent inflation prints in news window. Social Security COLA for 2027 may jump to 4.7% per one estimate, signaling persistent inflation concerns, but this is secondary to actual CPI/PCE releases due this week or next.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Breakevens
factors.summary: Market participants remain optimistic about middle-east stabilization, but underlying inflation anxiety persists (noted in analyst commentary on gold clearing $4,200). 5y5y breakevens likely stable; no deflationary shock evident.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (VIX, Equities)
factors.summary: Major indices closed higher on the day and week; SpaceX IPO success ($75B, +19% day-one) and Iran deal optimism fueled risk-on. However, gold also cleared $4,200 on Friday, suggesting dual safe-haven + real-yield support despite equity strength.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on GLD/IAU holdings or PBoC/RBI buying in this news cycle. Structural CB demand remains in place but not a near-term driver this week.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: No mention of Indian wedding season or Chinese holiday demand; out of primary seasonal bid windows for now.

screening.narrative: Gold has cleared and consolidated above $4,200 on Friday as market participants reconciled two opposing forces: (1) risk-off geopolitical de-escalation (Iran-US deal near signing, Hormuz reopening, oil down) which usually weighs on safe-haven demand, versus (2) persistent inflation anxiety and weakening USD as markets anticipate lower real yields if the Fed responds to falling oil/geopolitical premium with dovish bias. The 24h +0.66% gain and flatness in 4h (-0.04%) suggests the move is consolidating after a Phase 2 rally rather than extending. Upcoming central bank decisions (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA) and fresh inflation data (CPI/PCE likely this week) are critical wildcards that could flip the bias sharply.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision & dot plot (likely mid-late June per calendar)
US CPI/Core CPI release (scheduled early-mid June)
PCE/Core PCE (may be released this week or next)
BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (week of June 16-20)
NFP & ISM Manufacturing/Services (early/mid-week following)
Iran deal signing (next few days per Trump admin)
Oil market reaction to actual Hormuz supply flow changes
screening.risk_warning: If the Iran deal is formally signed AND holds, oil stabilizes below $85, and the Fed signals hawkish hold or rate hikes (not cuts) in response to sticky inflation data, gold could reverse sharply lower as real yields rise and USD strengthens. Conversely, if central banks pivot dovish or CPI surprises to the downside, gold could break higher past $4,250. The move is already +0.66% in 24h and consolidating, so late entries are higher-risk; wait for a pullback toward $4,215-$4,220 for better risk/reward into the central bank decision gauntlet.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation; if bullish bias holds, look for pullback retest of $4,215-$4,220 before considering fresh longs. Do not chase above $4,250 without confirmation of Fed dovishness or fresh CPI miss. Shorts risky until real yields data clarifies Fed stance post-Iran-deal.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating after a modest +0.66% 24h gain and +0.08% 4h move, with the prior BUY6/10 bias still in place but lacking fresh confirmation. The dominant macro driver today is **geopolitical de-escalation on Iran**: Trump administration signals a ceasefire deal is days away, which normally reduces safe-haven demand and can pressure gold. Offsetting this, the US Dollar remains slightly soft (~99.80 DXY), and real yields remain unknown in the current window—both potential supports. The week ahead is heavy on central bank decisions (BoJ, BoE, RBA) and economic data (retail sales, likely FOMC-adjacent volatility), creating chop risk and limiting conviction.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (this week, timing TBD)
US retail sales data (likely next 24-48h)
FOMC meeting or Powell speech (if scheduled this week)
Iran-US ceasefire agreement signing (Trump said 'coming days,' risk of disappointment or surprise hawkishness)
CPI/PCE inflation print (if scheduled this week; currently unknown)
screening.risk_warning: This bias could invalidate if: (1) Iran deal collapses or is postponed → sharp safe-haven rally in gold; (2) inflation surprise (hot CPI/PCE) comes in higher than expected → real yields spike, gold sold hard; (3) Fed Chair or major central banker signals hawkishness → DXY rallies, gold pressured; (4) equity selloff on any negative catalyst → gold could rally as safe-haven but also get caught in risk-off deleveraging of long positions.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE_2 consolidation after modest overnight gains; move is not extended but lacks fresh confirmation. Recommend watching for a retest of $4,230 support with volume confirmation before adding long, or waiting for a break above $4,250 resistance on fresh DXY weakness or real yield data. Avoid chasing into central bank decision week without clear trigger.

screening.narrative: Gold is stuck in tight consolidation ($4,238.80) with no major catalyst yet landed. The real yield regime is opaque — no TIPS, CPI, or Fed decision data in the past 24h. Geopolitical tensions (Iran-US war day 106, Israel-Lebanon) provide structural safe-haven support, but peace deal chatter and risk-on sentiment (SpaceX IPO euphoria) cap upside. Major central bank decisions (Fed, BoJ, BoE, RBA) are due imminently this week but results not yet in; this creates decision-tree uncertainty. Gold is neither decisively bullish nor bearish until real yields and DXY direction re-establish.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision (date TBD this week, likely Wed–Thu based on 'heavy central bank calendar')
BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions (week of June 13, dates unconfirmed in news)
US Retail Sales (likely mid-week)

ECB commentary (Nagel already leaning hawkish on inflation)
Iran-US peace deal finalization or escalation update (ongoing, high sensitivity)
USD movement into central bank week (often volatile)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed delivers hawkish surprise (higher terminal rate, faster normalization, or hawkish dot plot), real yields will spike and gold will face severe headwind. Conversely, if a dovish Fed + a new geopolitical flare-up (e.g., Israel-Iran direct confrontation) coincide, gold could break higher decisively. Current tight range is a chop zone; committing conviction before central bank clarity is unwise.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid entries until post-FOMC clarity. If gold closes above $4,260 on conviction post-Fed, consider long. If it breaks below $4,220, short-bias candidate, but only if DXY confirms (>100.00). For now, monitor from sidelines; price action lacks conviction.

screening.narrative: Gold is flat in intraday action (+0.05% 4h, +0.64% 24h) with minimal momentum despite macro backdrop. The dominant story is a shift toward risk-on: SpaceX's historic $75B IPO and Paramount-WBD DOJ approval signal strong equity/tech appetite, which typically competes with gold. Simultaneously, reports of a near-term US-Iran peace deal (Pakistan PM, 24h timeline) remove the geopolitical risk premium that has supported gold prices for months; however, ongoing Israeli strikes on Lebanon and Iranian funeral arrangements suggest the deal may be fragile or incomplete. Lacking new inflation data, Fed guidance, real yield direction, or explicit USD moves, gold lacks a clear macro anchor. The move is shallow and confirmation-less, aligning with prior NEUTRAL reads (4/10, 5/10, 6/10) from earlier on June 13.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal finalization (expected within 24 hours per Pakistan PM; if signed, likely bearish for gold geopolitical premium)
Lebanon/Israel escalation updates (ongoing; if widened, risk-on sentiment could reverse and favor gold)
Next US inflation print (CPI/PCE release date not in this window; check Fed calendar for exact timing)
FOMC decision / Powell speech (no date given in news; if scheduled soon, will cap confidence pending clarity)
ECB/BoE/BoJ meetings or commentary (ECB's Nagel commentary on sticky inflation suggests hawkish lean; could support USD/real yields, bearish gold)
BoJ policy or Yen carry unwind risk (SpaceX IPO risk-on may have unwound some yen-funded carry, but no explicit data)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal fully closes and geopolitical premium evaporates, gold likely tests lower support. Conversely, if deal fails or Lebanon escalates, gold could spike. Real yield direction (10y TIPS) is the critical missing data—if TIPS yields are falling, gold should be stronger; if rising, gold is at risk. Current flat price action suggests market is uncertain; a major inflation print, Fed surprise, or deal confirmation in the next 24-72h will likely force resolution and break the chop.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing. Price action is minimal and directionless (0.05% in 4h). Wait for either: (1) Iran deal confirmation → likely gold selloff, enter shorts on relief rally; (2) inflation surprise or Fed dovish signal → likely gold bounce, enter longs on retest of $4,220 support. Next 24h is critical; do not enter until a major catalyst resolves the NEUTRAL bias.

screening.narrative: Gold is trading in a tight range (+0.05% in 4h, +0.64% in 24h) with no clear directional thrust. The dominant macro story is dual-track: (1) Iran-US peace deal talks moving closer (potential dovish catalyst for gold via lower real yields and reduced geopolitical premium), but (2) simultaneous Israel-Lebanon escalation creating offsetting safe-haven demand. Meanwhile, risk sentiment is buoyed by SpaceX IPO success and M&A approvals, which typically competes with gold flows. Real yields and DXY remain opaque in this news cycle, preventing high-conviction directional bias. Pimco's warning on debt-market defaults and stretched equities hints at latent fragility, but not yet translated into gold buying.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal finalization (could occur within 24h per Pakistan PM statement; dovish for gold if successful, bullish if talks collapse)
Israel-Lebanon military developments (ongoing, unpredictable; any escalation = safe-haven bid)

screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US deal is finalized and holds without further escalation, safe-haven premium evaporates and gold could sell off sharply. Conversely, if real yields fall sharply or DXY weakens materially, gold could break higher. Current neutral bias is vulnerable to either tail. Data dependency is extreme: next inflation print or Fed communication could easily flip the bias 2–3 points in either direction. Absence of clear real-yield and dollar signals makes this a 'show-me' environment; do not override with conviction until TIPs or DXY gives direction.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in Phase 1 (fresh, minimal move underway). Avoid entry on thesis alone until catalyst clarity or fresh data. Wait for IR breakout above $4,260 (resistance) with volume OR pullback to $4,225 (support) + confirmation of underlying macro (real yield or DXY signal). Iran deal outcome in next 24h is the primary pivot; stand aside until resolution or clear break of technical levels.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a de-escalation trap. The dominant news story is the imminent Iran-US peace deal (signed Sunday per Trump), which removes a major geopolitical risk premium and reignites risk-on sentiment—evidenced by SpaceX's historic 19% IPO pop and Bitcoin breaking above $64k. Simultaneously, real yields remain unshaken (no hawkish Fed move, no inflation surprise), and the USD has no directional tailwind. The 24h +0.64% gain appears to be a carry-over from Friday but lacks fundamental support for extension higher. Expect chop into Sunday's deal-signing event.
catalysts_ahead: Trump Iran peace deal signing (Sunday, June 15, 2026) — if signed as announced, expect further de-risk and potential gold pullback
Strait of Hormuz reopening (implicit in Iran deal) — bearish for oil, inflation expectations, and gold
Next CPI/PCE release (likely mid-late June) — critical for real-yield and Fed-rate expectations
FOMC meeting / Powell speech (date not specified in feed, likely mid-June) — monetary policy guidance
World Cup momentum / consumer sentiment tracking (second-order macro effect)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal COLLAPSES or is delayed beyond Sunday, geopolitical premium re-escalates sharply—gold would spike $40–$80 above $4,240. Conversely, if inflation data (CPI/PCE) comes in hotter than expected in coming weeks, real yields could fall despite de-escalation, supporting gold. Fed pivot to easing (unlikely near-term but possible if recession fears rise) would be deeply bullish for gold. Monitor DXY and TIPS breakevens closely for confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase; current price shows minimal intraday conviction (+0.05% in 4h). Wait for either (1) pullback toward $4,200–$4,210 support if de-risk continues, or (2) a break above $4,260 with fresh inflation/Fed catalyst before adding longs. Shorts risk Sunday deal signing; avoid aggressive shorts into event risk.

screening.narrative: Gold remains range-bound at $4,238.80, caught between competing narratives: (1) Geopolitical uncertainty (Ukraine, lingering Middle East tensions) provides modest safe-haven floor; (2) US-Iran deal optimism (deal 'signed Sunday') is de-risking sentiment, boosting equities/crypto and pressuring gold safe-haven premium; (3) Absent fresh inflation data, Fed signals, or real-yield moves, gold has no catalyst to break higher. Risk appetite dominance (SpaceX mega-IPO, Bitcoin $64k+) is currently the dominant regime, pulling flows away from bullion into risk assets. With no major economic data or Fed commentary today, directional conviction is low.

screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal closes successfully over weekend without renegotiation, geopolitical safe-haven bid could evaporate quickly, pushing gold lower to $4,200–$4,180. Conversely, if deal collapses or fresh Iran-Israel flare-up occurs, gold could spike to $4,300+. Major inflation surprise or hawkish Fed signal (if due) would be highly bearish. Real yields ticking higher (even 5–10bps) could accelerate decline given low current momentum. Risk sentiment fully dominates; any equity/crypto pullback would be immediate gold tailwind.
screening.entry_guidance: Consolidation mode—no clear entry. On break above $4,255 with VIX <13, consider small short scalp targeting $4,220 (geopolitical floor). On break below $4,220 with deal closed, short trend to $4,180. WAIT for either (a) deal closure + capitulation, (b) fresh inflation/Fed catalyst, or (c) equity sell-off to generate meaningful gold move. Current range is noisy; do not chase.

screening.narrative: Gold is stuck in a holding pattern with +0.64% 24h gain barely above session noise (+0.05% 4h). The dominant macro driver is Iran-US peace deal speculation, which de-escalates geopolitical risk premium and should be BEARISH for gold, yet headlines are still contradictory (Trump says Sunday, Iran says 'not tomorrow' but 'close'). Simultaneously, SpaceX IPO +19% and Bitcoin rebounding show risk-on sentiment kicking in—typically bearish for safe-haven gold. However, NO fresh inflation data, Fed policy, or real-yield updates exist in the past 24h, leaving the core price drivers opaque. Three consecutive NEUTRAL 4/10 reads confirm market chop and lack of directional conviction.

screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US deal is confirmed signed and Strait of Hormuz reopens, oil crashes, and geopolitical premium collapses—gold could see sharp 1-2% selloff. Conversely, if deal collapses or new Middle East escalation emerges, gold would spike. Major unknown: real yields and Fed pivot signals remain absent. Without fresh inflation or Fed data, gold is directionless and chop-prone; avoid forced long/short bias into weekend.
screening.entry_guidance: NEUTRAL 4/10 — avoid entries. Market lacks conviction and catalysts (Iran deal timing unclear, no inflation data). If forced: enter only on confirmed deal signing news (BEARISH short ~$4,220 support breach) OR on new geopolitical escalation (BULLISH long at $4,260 resistance breakout). Weekend chop expected; wait for Monday and next macro data.
